About Us

About The Palms Surgery

Providing comprehensive, compassionate healthcare to the people of Wexford and Wicklow since 1953.

Our Practice

Your Health is Our Partnership

We are delighted to welcome you to our practice. It is our aim to provide you and your family with a comprehensive general practice service, in an efficient and friendly manner.

We strive to provide a clean, safe and comfortable clinic. Our clinical staff aim to provide you with appropriate and timely care. We respect your privacy and the confidentiality of your medical and personal information.

We treat our partnership with you as a personal relationship. We believe that the more effort we put in to taking care of your health, the more you will benefit. This is why we ask you to participate as much as possible regarding your care, and to let us know if you have any concerns or if you do not understand any information that we give you.

Your opinions matter very much to us. If you have any comments, suggestions or complaints about our service, please let us know.

🔒 Confidentiality
Your medical and personal information is strictly protected and never shared without consent.
♿ Accessibility
Our practice is fully accessible. We provide interpreting services where needed.
📋 Health Literacy
We are committed to clear, plain-language communication with all patients.
🌍 Community
We are an active community practice with global health commitments through our Malawi Partnership.

Get in Touch

If you have any questions, concerns or suggestions, we'd love to hear from you.

Our Team

Physician Associate

Our Physician Associate works alongside our GP team to provide high-quality patient care across a range of clinical areas.

Maria Smith
Physician Associate
Working alongside our GP team across a range of clinical areas including the Menopause Clinic.
Role

What is a Physician Associate?

A Physician Associate (PA) is a healthcare professional who works alongside doctors to provide medical care. PAs are trained to take patient histories, perform examinations, interpret investigations, make diagnoses and develop management plans.

At The Palms Surgery, Maria is involved in the Menopause Clinic, assisting with initial patient assessments, taking measurements and organising blood tests prior to the doctor review.

Services Provided by Maria

✓Initial health assessments and history-taking
✓Physical examinations and clinical measurements
✓Organising and interpreting investigations
✓Supporting the Menopause Clinic
✓Chronic disease reviews
✓Patient education and health advice

⚧ Male and Female Health

  • STI Tests for Sexually Transmitted Diseases
  • Blood Tests for HIV, Syphilis, Hepatitis A & B
  • Swabs or Urine Tests for Chlamydia and Gonorrhoea
  • Prostate Gland Examination
  • Breast Examination
  • Testicular Examination
  • Mole Checks
  • Cervical Smears

🔬 Health Screening

  • Full Medical Health Check
  • Blood Tests for Diabetes
  • Hypertension / 24 Hour Blood Pressure Monitoring
  • Resting ECG
  • Blood Tests for Raised Cholesterol

📋 Health Examinations For

  • Driving Licences

Services

Minor Surgery &
Joint Injections

We provide a comprehensive range of minor surgical procedures at The Palms Surgery — fast, cost-efficient and done in-practice.

⚕️ Procedures We Offer

  • Abscess Drainage
  • Stitching of Wounds in Emergencies
  • Removal of Foreign Bodies (e.g. Timber, Glass)
  • Non-Melanoma Skin Cancer Biopsy and Removal
  • Removal of Sebaceous Cysts
  • Joint Injections
  • Removal of Skin Tags
  • Cryotherapy (Freezing) — Warts, Verrucae and other Skin Lesions
  • Ingrown Toenail Removal
⚠️ Important Note
All surgical procedures require a pre-surgical assessment by appointment before the procedure can be carried out. Please contact the surgery to arrange your assessment.

Book a Pre-Surgical Assessment

A pre-surgical assessment is required before any procedure. This is a brief consultation with your doctor to discuss the procedure and ensure you are suitable.

📞 053 942 1336
🕐 Mon–Fri: 8:30am – 5:00pm
Specialist Clinic

Menopause Clinic

Compassionate, personalised specialist care for every stage of the menopause journey — led by Dr Boselli and Dr Lima.

About the Clinic

Specialist Menopause Care

Menopause occurs when your periods stop, due to lower hormone levels. This normally happens between the ages of 45 and 55, but can vary for each person.

Dr Carlotta Boselli and Dr Carla Lima run a specialist clinic to help you manage symptoms caused by the menopause. This is a private service and is not covered by the medical card — please contact the surgery for pricing information.

🌸 How the Clinic Works

  • Initial consultation with our Physician Associate for measurements and blood tests
  • Blood test results reviewed by Dr Boselli or Dr Lima
  • Full discussion of your personal symptoms and issues
  • Tailored treatment plan formulated to your specific needs
  • Follow-up review arranged approximately three months later
  • Ongoing support and plan adjustments as needed

💊 What We Can Help With

  • Hot flushes and night sweats
  • Mood changes, anxiety and low mood
  • Sleep disturbances
  • HRT prescribing and management
  • Bone health (osteoporosis prevention)
  • Perimenopause symptoms
  • Premature ovarian insufficiency (POI)
💳 Private Service
Please note this is a private service and is not covered by the medical card. Please contact the surgery for pricing details.

Book a Menopause Consultation

Contact us to arrange your initial consultation. We will explain the full process and what to expect.

TMB Accredited

Travel Clinic

Part of the Tropical Medical Bureau (TMB) network — offering specialised travel vaccinations and comprehensive health advice for all destinations.

About Our Travel Clinic

Expert Travel Health

The Palms GP Surgery is part of the well-known TMB group, offering specialised travel clinics, comprehensive travel vaccinations and health advice programmes as part of the Tropical Medical Bureau (www.tmb.ie).

Our TMB-dedicated healthcare professionals are trained and skilled in tropical medicine. Three of our doctors have worked in Africa, and the clinic is linked to two hospitals in Malawi to provide training in chronic disease management.

⏰ When Should I Get Vaccinated?

  • Most travel vaccinations required at least 4 weeks prior to travel
  • Some courses of injections may take up to 6 weeks
  • Hepatitis B ideally needs 6 months to complete a course
  • Children travelling require special consideration — speak to us at least 6 weeks prior
🌍 Beyond Vaccination
Travel medicine does not stop at vaccination. We also provide comprehensive advice regarding malaria prevention, sanitation, general health precautions and risk avoidance for your specific destination.
Available Vaccines
Diphtheria
Polio
Tetanus
Typhoid
Hepatitis A
Hepatitis B
Tetanus / Diphtheria
Yellow Fever
Rabies
Meningitis ACYN-135
Varicella (chickenpox)
Cholera (oral)
Influenza
Pneumococcal
Whooping cough

Book a Travel Consultation

Please allow plenty of time before travel. Contact us as soon as you know your travel dates.

Global Health

Gorey Malawi Health Partnership

Improving clinical care and education for chronic diseases in Malawi — a partnership formed in May 2016 between The Palms Surgery and healthcare professionals in Northern Malawi.

2016
Partnership Founded
16M
People in Malawi
3
Doctors with Africa Experience
Why Malawi?

The Need

The Gorey-Malawi Health Partnership was formed in May 2016 and is the clinical partner of the gHealth Research Group.

Following a detailed needs assessment in 2016, chronic diseases (non-communicable diseases, or NCDs) were identified as a critical area of need. The WHO estimates that 16 million people die prematurely worldwide from heart and lung diseases, stroke, cancer and diabetes — with nearly three quarters of these deaths occurring in low and middle income countries.

Malawi is one of the poorest countries in the world, with a population of 16 million. A 2009 WHO survey found that 33% of people aged 25–64 had high blood pressure, yet 75% were unaware. An estimated 5.6% of the population have diabetes and 5.1% have asthma, with no programmes for prevention or control.

🎯 Our Goals

  • Develop research and practical strategies for chronic disease management
  • Focus initially on asthma — identified as a priority by Malawian partners
  • Cover two areas: Mzuzu (major city) and Livingstonia (rural) in Northern Malawi
  • Enable expansion to heart disease, diabetes and hypertension
  • Build long-term capacity with local healthcare workers
What We've Done

Progress & Activities

Since the partnership formed, significant progress has been made:

✅ Milestones

  • Two visits made to Malawi by our medical team
  • Reciprocal visits to The Palms Surgery from the Director of NCDs in the Malawian Ministry of Health
  • Visit from the Director of Education from Mzuzu Central Hospital
  • Clinic linked to two hospitals in Malawi for chronic disease training
  • Three Palms GPs have worked in Africa
  • Academic partnerships developed in Europe

Get Involved

If you would like to support the Gorey Malawi Health Partnership or find out more, please get in touch with us.

Physiotherapy

Physiotherapy &
Exercise Guides

Our team may direct you here for guided exercises to help with injuries and common musculoskeletal issues. Select your area below.

🤲

Wrist Flexion & Extension

Gently bend your wrist up and down slowly. Keep your forearm still on a flat surface. Stop if you feel sharp pain.

3 sets × 10 reps
🔄

Wrist Rotation

Rotate your wrist in slow circles — 5 clockwise, then 5 anti-clockwise. Perform gently and within a comfortable range.

3 sets × 10 reps
👊

Grip Strengthening

Squeeze a soft ball or rolled towel firmly in your hand, hold for 5 seconds, then release slowly.

3 sets × 10 reps
↔️

Elbow Flexion & Extension

Bend and straighten the elbow slowly through its full comfortable range of motion. Can be done seated or standing.

3 sets × 10 reps
🖐️

Finger Spread

Spread fingers as wide as possible, hold for 3 seconds, then bring together. Helps with hand and wrist coordination.

3 sets × 10 reps
💆

Wrist Stretch

Extend arm with palm facing up, gently pull fingers back with other hand until you feel a stretch in the forearm. Hold for 20 seconds.

3 × 20 second holds
🦶

Ankle Alphabet

Trace the letters of the alphabet with your big toe, moving only your ankle. Great for improving range of motion.

1–2 times daily
⬆️

Calf Raises

Stand near a wall for balance. Slowly rise up on your toes and lower back down. Build strength gradually.

3 sets × 15 reps
🔄

Ankle Circles

Rotate your ankle in circles — 10 clockwise, then 10 anti-clockwise. Do this seated with your leg elevated if possible.

3 sets × 10 each way
🧘

Towel Stretch

Sit on the floor, loop a towel around your foot, and gently pull toes towards you. Hold for a stretch in the calf.

3 × 30 second holds
⚖️

Single Leg Balance

Stand on one leg near a wall for safety. Hold for 20–30 seconds. Progress by closing your eyes as strength improves.

3 × 30 second holds
↕️

Toe Curls

While seated, place a small towel flat on the floor and scrunch it up using your toes. This strengthens the intrinsic foot muscles.

3 sets × 10 reps
💪

Pendulum Swings

Lean forward slightly, let affected arm hang down. Gently swing in small circles. Relieves tension and improves mobility.

3 sets × 30 seconds
🔼

Shoulder Shrugs

Slowly raise both shoulders up towards ears, hold for 5 seconds, then slowly lower. Great for releasing tension.

3 sets × 10 reps
🦋

Wall Crawl

Face a wall, walk fingers up slowly as high as is comfortable, hold briefly, then walk back down. Improves range of motion.

3 sets × 10 reps
🧘

Cross-Body Stretch

Bring one arm across your chest. Use opposite hand to gently hold at elbow and increase the stretch. Hold.

3 × 30 second holds
↔️

Internal Rotation

Use a resistance band or towel behind your back. Gently rotate arm inward, hold, release. Builds rotator cuff strength.

3 sets × 10 reps
🔙

Shoulder Blade Squeeze

Sit or stand upright. Squeeze shoulder blades together as if holding a pencil between them. Hold for 5 seconds.

3 sets × 10 reps
⚠️ Important Guidance
These exercises are for guidance only. If you experience significant pain during any exercise, stop immediately and contact the surgery. A member of our team may have directed you here — please follow their specific advice and instructions.
Online Booking

Book Your Appointment

No waiting on hold. Choose your appointment type, pick a time, and confirm instantly. Available Mon–Fri, 8:30am–5:00pm.

How It Works

Simple 4-Step Process

Booking online is quick and easy. Here's what to expect:

1

Choose Appointment Type

Select from GP consultation, nurse, specialist clinic, or physio session.

2

Pick a Date & Time

See real-time availability and select a slot that works for your schedule.

3

Enter Your Details

Provide your contact details and a brief reason for your visit.

4

Instant Confirmation

Receive an email and SMS confirmation with full appointment details.

📞 Prefer to Call?
You can also book by phone: 053 942 1336
Mon–Fri 8:30am – 5:00pm
🚨 Not for Emergencies
For medical emergencies, call 999. Out-of-hours urgent care: CAREDOC 0818 300 365
